rulings.Case Title: MARY RAGO VS. WOOD STREET PLAZA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT SAMSON LAI, SUSAN LAI, YOSEMITE CAPITAL, LLC, ERRONEOUSLY SUED HEREIN AS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions Objections to Roeder Declaration are sustained. Defendant's Motion for Terminating Sanctions is GRANTED and stayed for (1) no later than 5:00 p.m. on April 16, 2021 Jerry Heath to deliver to defendant Yosemite, without objections, the responses as ordered on November 3, 2020; (2) Jerry Heath to appear for a remote deposition at 10:00 a.m. on April 23, 2021 (or other mutually agreeable date no later than April 30, 2021. Sanctions granted (without stay) in the amount of $5,551.20 to be paid within 10 days of this order. If a proceeding for determination of Mr. Heath competency is initiated by April 13, 2021, Mr. Heath may appear ex-parte no later than April 15, 2021 to obtain a further stay of the order re: terminating sanctions the determination of competency is rendered. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DEMETRICE JONES VS. DENISE WILLIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANT DENISE WILLIS DEMURRER to COMPLAINT sustained with leave to amend. Complaint in this action is uncertain as Plaintiff appears to allege that there is no landlord-tenant relationship (see notice terminating a license) and that a tenancy was terminated for just cause (see form UD-101 10(a)). Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to reconcile these allegations. If UD is based on just cause termination of tenancy all elements of such cause of action shall be alleged. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
